title.en: Build faster with a production-ready foundation

children.text: This starterkit provides reusable content blocks, seeded sample content, and a clean baseline for Payload CMS projects.

header.text: A foundation you can build on

children.text: Out of the box you get configurable page blocks, story content patterns, a seeded navigation structure, and production-focused defaults. Every piece is designed to be replaced or extended as your project grows.

description.en: Hero, standard content, icon rows, image grids, story cards, and more — all configurable from the admin panel.
Four role levels — super admin, website admin, editor, and reader — with fine-grained access control baked in.
Multi-locale support with next-intl. English, French, Spanish, Arabic, and Russian included by default.

header.text: Open source and ready to deploy

children.text: Fork the repo, configure your environment, and ship — the hard parts are already handled.

meta.title: Payload CMS Starterkit
meta.description: A generic starterkit for Payload CMS web applications.

children.text: This repository is intended as a reusable baseline. It favors clear structure, editable blocks, and practical defaults so teams can start shipping quickly.

The starterkit is maintained as an opinionated base. It makes specific choices — Next.js App Router, Payload v3, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS — so you spend less time on setup and more time on your product.

description.en: Generated TypeScript types from your Payload schema. Never guess a field name again.
Server components, static generation, and Next.js Image optimization configured out of the box.
Every block and collection is independently replaceable. Use what fits, delete what doesn't.

children.text: Clone the repository, copy .env.example to .env, run the database seed, then start the development server. The admin panel is at /admin. Default seed credentials are printed during the seed run.

meta.title: About
meta.description: Information about the starterkit's goals and structure.

children.text: Use this page as a placeholder for project-specific contact details, forms, and support links.
Send us an email and we'll get back to you within one business day.

body.en: Hi, I'd like to get in touch.

description.en: Open issues, submit PRs, or browse the source code on GitHub.
Join the Payload community Discord for questions and discussion.
Full Payload CMS documentation is available at payloadcms.com.

children.text: This is a starter template privacy page. Replace this content with your organization-specific privacy disclosures before launch.
This is a starter template terms page. Replace this content with legal terms that apply to your deployed product.
Every content block available in this starterkit, demonstrated on one page.
This is the simple-rich-text block. It supports a title, background color, and a full Lexical rich text body. Use it for editorial prose, long-form copy, or explanatory sections.
The standard-content block places an image beside rich text. The image can float left or right. The header field supports per-word highlight colors and custom heading levels.

description.en: Each icon-row item has an icon name (Remix Icons), background color, title, description, and an optional link button.
Up to three items per row. This block is ideal for feature lists, value propositions, and benefit summaries.
Icons come from the Remix Icon library. Pass the icon name without the 'ri-' prefix.

children.text: The wide-image block fills the full container width with an image. The header and optional body copy are overlaid or placed adjacent depending on the layout.
Info Block — theme: info. Use this for informational callouts. Supports link and email action types.
Info Block — theme: success. Use this for positive confirmations and completion states.
